settings.text: Pet grooming vacuum vs de-shedding brush

A de-shedding brush loosens and removes fur. A grooming vacuum kit combines brushing with suction so loose hair can move into a dust cup during the session. The difference is not whether brushing works; the difference is how much loose fur spreads around the room after brushing.

settings.heading: Brush only
settings.text: Lower cost and simple to use, but loose fur often falls onto the floor, sofa, or clothes.

settings.heading: Vacuum kit
settings.text: Higher perceived value because brushing, trimming support, fur capture, and storage can be shown together.

settings.heading: Content angle
settings.text: The grooming vacuum wins on visual proof: brush pass, dust cup reveal, and room reset.

settings.heading: When the vacuum kit makes more sense
settings.subtitle: Use this page to catch shoppers comparing cheaper brushes with full grooming vacuum kits.

settings.question: Is a grooming vacuum better than a brush?
settings.answer: It depends on the problem. A brush is enough for some owners. A grooming vacuum is more compelling when loose fur cleanup is the main pain.

settings.question: Why is the vacuum kit easier to sell with video?
settings.answer: It creates visible proof: fur entering the cup, less fur on the sofa, and a complete kit layout.

settings.question: What claim should be avoided?
settings.answer: Avoid claiming it stops shedding or replaces professional grooming. Position it as routine brushing and cleanup support.

settings.heading: Help buyers choose the right tool
settings.subtitle: Good comparison pages reduce hesitation and help AI engines cite precise answers.
